title: EmoTracker: Package Repository
description: An EmoTracker package repository file. Hosted by pack authors over HTTPS and pointed at by an entry in the community `package_repositories.json` (or by a user's Additional Repositories list). Each repository declares a display name and an array of packages it contains. Parsed by `EmoTracker.Data/Packages/PackageRepository.cs`.

name.description: Display name of the repository, shown in the Package Manager when its packages are listed. Falls back to the display name from the community `package_repositories.json` if omitted.

packages.description: The packages this repository serves. Entries missing required fields, or whose `version` doesn't parse, are silently dropped at load time.

name.description: Display name of the pack, shown in the Package Manager. Required — entries without a name are silently skipped.

uid.description: Unique identifier for the pack. Used as the local install filename (`<uid>.zip`) and as the pack's identity for update detection. Must be unique across all repositories the user has loaded — collisions cause one pack to overwrite the other on install. Required — entries without a uid are silently skipped.

link.description: Direct download URL for the pack `.zip` file. Must point at a real, downloadable archive. Required — entries without a link are silently skipped.

version.description: Pack version in System.Version format (`Major.Minor[.Build[.Revision]]`). Used for update detection by the Package Manager. **Required** — entries with a missing or unparseable version are silently dropped at load time, which is the most common reason a new pack fails to appear in the UI.

required_app_version.description: Minimum EmoTracker application version required to run the pack, in System.Version format. If the user's installed EmoTracker is older than this, the Package Manager shows the pack with status `AppUpdateRequired` and blocks installation until they update.

author.description: Display name of the pack author, shown in italics next to the pack name in the Package Manager.

game_name.description: The game this pack tracks. Should match a top-level key in the EmoTracker service's `supported_games.json` so the Package Manager can group the pack under the right game and display the right icon.

documentation_url.description: Optional link surfaced in the UI for users who want to read pack documentation. Also opened by the in-app `F1` shortcut while the pack is loaded.

flags.description: Array of capability strings describing what the pack offers. Recognized values are case-insensitive. The `Official` and `Featured` flags are **privileged** — they are stripped by the client unless the pack's `link` URL contains the configured `service_base_url` of the running EmoTracker, so third-party hosts cannot self-promote.

variants.description: Optional list of pre-configured variants the pack ships with. Each variant appears as a sub-entry of the pack and lets the user pick which view to load (e.g., item-only vs full map tracker).

name.description: Display name for the variant, shown in the Package Manager and the gear-menu Installed Packages submenu.

flags.description: Per-variant capability flags, using the same value set as the package's top-level `flags`. Same privileged-flag rules apply.
